context: Brainstorm of improvements to Merill's scraping and clustering pipeline.

grouping.title: Use article body, not just headline + snippet
grouping.problem: HTML-scraped publishers (Malta Today, Independent, TVM) have empty body_snippet, so RSS articles cluster better than HTML ones — uneven by source.
grouping.proposal: Fetch body text per article and add a body bag-of-words at lower weight (~0.3x) to the TF-IDF input.
grouping.tradeoff: Extra HTTP per article; mitigate with lazy/queued fetching after initial cluster assignment.

grouping.title: Maltese-specific stop words + tokenizer
grouping.problem: STOP_WORDS is English-only. MT articles leak tokens like 'ta', 'il', 'tal' through the !is_alphanumeric splitter; apostrophes (f'Malta) break tokenization.
grouping.proposal: Add MT stop list; strip leading-article prefixes (il-, it-, ix-, ta', tal-, tas-, f') before tokenizing.

grouping.title: Category gating
grouping.problem: Sport and politics articles can theoretically cluster if vocabulary overlaps.
grouping.proposal: Refuse to consider candidate clusters whose category differs from the new article's category.
grouping.tradeoff: Misclassified articles get isolated; mitigate by allowing 'general' category to match anything.

grouping.title: URL slug as additional clustering signal
grouping.problem: SEO slugs (/news/labour-leader-resigns-after-…) are dense, descriptive, and ignored by current clustering.
grouping.proposal: Tokenize the URL slug and merge into cluster text at weight ~1.5x; robust to translation mismatches.
grouping.tradeoff: Some publishers use opaque URLs (numeric IDs); skip when slug has <2 word-tokens.

grouping.title: Lightweight NER instead of capitalisation heuristic
grouping.problem: Current proper-noun rule misses sentence-initial entities and trips on mid-sentence capitals.
grouping.proposal: Rule-based pass with known Malta entities (parties: PL, PN, ADPD; people: Abela, Grech; institutions: MEPA, Transport Malta) + capitalised-bigram grouping.
grouping.tradeoff: Entity list needs occasional manual updates; can also be derived from publisher metadata.

grouping.title: Cluster centroid + same-publisher threshold bump
grouping.problem: Greedy O(n) iteration over every headline per cluster; same-publisher articles can join one cluster, undermining the cross-publisher product premise.
grouping.proposal: (a) Maintain a mean TF-IDF vector per cluster, score against centroid. (b) Raise threshold slightly when candidate cluster already contains the article's publisher.
grouping.tradeoff: Centroids smooth out outliers — keep per-headline scoring as fallback for borderline scores.

grouping.title: Second-pass cluster-to-cluster merge
grouping.problem: Greedy single-pass means an article that arrives before its companions seeds a singleton that should have merged.
grouping.proposal: After greedy assignment, run centroid-vs-centroid sweep and merge clusters above a high threshold (~0.55).
grouping.tradeoff: Risk of over-merging; gate by minimum-shared-anchor requirement.

grouping.title: Time-aware similarity boost
grouping.problem: Stale clusters are penalised, but fresh-vs-fresh proximity is not rewarded.
grouping.proposal: Multiplicative boost: score *= 1 + 0.2 * exp(-Δh/12) — favours clusters within ~6h of the new article.
grouping.tradeoff: Tune decay constant against real refresh data.

grouping.title: Optional embedding tie-breaker for ambiguous matches
grouping.problem: Lexical TF-IDF can't bridge synonyms ('Cabinet approves law' vs 'Government passes legislation').
grouping.proposal: When TF-IDF score lands in 0.18–0.34 gray zone, run small on-device sentence encoder (MiniLM-L6 ONNX, ~80MB, ~5ms/headline) as tie-breaker.
grouping.tradeoff: Adds dependency + binary size; only used for ambiguous candidates so latency cost is bounded.

grouping.title: Adaptive threshold by cluster size
grouping.problem: Constant threshold lets large clusters become magnets and prevents fledgling clusters from accreting.
grouping.proposal: Mature clusters (≥5 articles) raise threshold by ~0.05; tentative clusters (1–2) lower by ~0.03.
grouping.tradeoff: Hyperparameters need tuning; verify with a labelled subset.

grouping.title: Enrich MT clustering text with original entity tokens
grouping.problem: If translator drops/mangles proper nouns, anchor matching for MT articles dies.
grouping.proposal: Append proper nouns and numbers from the MT original alongside the EN translation when building cluster text.
grouping.tradeoff: Slightly noisier vocabulary; offset by stop-word filter.

scraping.title: URL canonicalisation before stable_id
scraping.problem: Raw URL hash means ?utm_source=…, #comments, trailing slash, ?replytocom= variants create duplicate article IDs.
scraping.proposal: Strip query params (allowlist of meaningful ones), fragments, normalise scheme/host case, and trailing slash before hashing.
scraping.tradeoff: Some publishers use query params for legitimate IDs (?p=123); allowlist them.

scraping.title: Replace string-based sitemap parser with quick-xml
scraping.problem: split('<url>') + substring searches break on namespaced tags, multi-line attributes, and CDATA edge cases — silent drops.
scraping.proposal: Switch to quick-xml streaming parser; preserve structure and handle CDATA/namespaces.

scraping.title: Conditional GET (ETag / If-Modified-Since)
scraping.problem: RSS feeds rarely change between 15-min refetches; we re-download the full body each time.
scraping.proposal: Persist last ETag / Last-Modified per source; send the conditional headers; treat 304 as 'no new articles'.
scraping.tradeoff: Schema migration to store headers; minimal.

scraping.title: Per-host concurrency limit + per-future timeout
scraping.problem: join_all blasts all publishers in parallel — one slow site holds up the whole user-visible refresh.
scraping.proposal: Wrap each fetch in tokio::time::timeout (~10s); cap concurrency at ~5 with a Semaphore.
scraping.tradeoff: Slightly slower in the happy path; predictable worst case.

scraping.title: Per-publisher health metrics + selector auto-fallback
scraping.problem: Static selectors silently break when sites redesign; auto_detect_article_sel exists but only runs for custom publishers.
scraping.proposal: Track scraped count per publisher; if 0 or <expected/3 for two consecutive refreshes, retry via auto_detect_article_sel and surface 'stale source' to the UI.
scraping.tradeoff: Need a baseline/expected count per publisher; can be a rolling median.

scraping.title: Readability-style article body extraction
scraping.problem:
Fixed container selectors +

-length heuristic miss reformatted layouts.
scraping.proposal: Use dom_smoothie or readable-readability crate; powers idea #1 (body in clustering).
scraping.tradeoff: Adds a dependency; consider feature-flagging while validating.

scraping.title: Don't fake published_at on bad sitemap dates
scraping.problem: Falling back to Utc::now() makes parse-failed articles look artificially fresh and skews stale-cluster checks.
scraping.proposal: Skip the article, or carry None and exclude from time-sensitive logic.
scraping.tradeoff: Some real articles get dropped; logging will reveal frequency.

scraping.title: Size-aware image selection + cluster-level best image
scraping.problem: First 'good' image wins; placeholders sometimes appear first. UI shows inconsistent imagery across articles in a cluster.
scraping.proposal: Parse og:image:width/srcset widths, prefer ≥600px; choose a single cluster-level image (largest or from highest-scored publisher).
scraping.tradeoff: More HTML parsing; bounded since we already iterate .
